sensitivity: 88.47% (95% CI: 86.38%; 90.28%) for standalone; 89.35% (95% CI: 88.36%; 90.27%) for aided reader performance
specificity: 82.94% (95% CI: 80.66%; 90.28%) for standalone; 85.10% (95% CI: 83.96%; 90.27%) for aided reader performance
auc: 0.8408 (95% Bootstrap CI: 0.8272; 0.8548) for standalone; 0.8583 for aided reader performance
intended_use: Rayvolve LN is a computer-aided detection software device to assist radiologists to identify and mark regions in relation to suspected pulmonary nodules from 6 to 30mm size. It is designed to aid radi
intended_use_full: Rayvolve LN is a computer-aided detection software device to assist radiologists to identify and mark regions in relation to suspected pulmonary nodules from 6 to 30mm size. It is designed to aid radiologists in reviewing the frontal (AP/PA) chest radiographs of patients of 18 years of age or older acquired on digital radiographic systems as a second reader and be used with any DICOM Node server. Rayvolve LN provides adjunctive information only and is not a substitute for the original chest radiographic image.
target_condition: Suspected pulmonary nodules on chest radiographs
ground_truth: Binary labeling indicating presence or absence of pulmonary nodules from validation dataset; radiologist consensus implied through MRMC study design
other_metrics: AFROC (Alternative Free Response Receiver Operating Characteristic) and False Positives Per Image reported; Reader AUC improved from 0.8071 (unaided) to 0.8583 (aided), difference of 0.0511 (95% CI: 0.0501; 0.0518)
